title: Poongatru Thirumbuma Lyrics Muthal Mariyathai
stanzas:
Poongaathu thirumbuma
emmpaatta virumbumaa
paaraatta madiyil vachi
thaalaatta
enakoru thaai madi
kidaikkumaa

Raasaave varuthama
Raasaave varuthama
aagaayam surungumaa
yengaatha adha
ulagam thaangaathe
adukkumaa sooriyan
karukkumaa

enna solluven
en ullam thaangala
metha vaangunen
thookkatha vaangala

indha vedhana
yaarukkuthaan illa.
unna meerave
ooril aal illa

suga raagam sogamthaane
suga raagam sogamthaane
yaar adhu poradhu
kuyil paadalaam
than mugam kaattumaa

Poongaathu thirumbuma
emmpaatta virumbumaa
paaraatta madiyil vachi
thaalaatta
enakoru thaai madi
kidaikkumaa

maane en nenjuku
paal vaartha thene
munne en paarvaiku
vaa vaa penne

yesa paattu padichen
naane
yesa paattu padichen
naane
poongiyil yaaradhu
konjam paarunga
pen kuyil naanunga

adi neethaanaa
andha kuyil
yaar veettu sondha kuyil
aathaadi manasukulla
kaathaadi
parandhathen
ulagame marandhathen

naanthaane andha kuyil
thaanaaga vandha kuyil
aathaadi
manasukul kaathaadi
parandhadhaa
ulagamthaan marandhathaa

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses deep emotions of love and longing, reflecting the complexities of relationships.

What is the context of the song in the movie?

In Muthal Mariyathai, the song plays during a pivotal moment that highlights the romantic tension between the lead characters.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song features the melodious voices of S. Janaki and Malaysia Vasudevan.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Poongatru Thirumbuma was composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja.

When was this song released?

The song was released on August 15, 1985.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Poongatru Thirumbuma, the lyrical beauty penned by Vairamuthu intertwines with the soulful voices of S. Janaki and Malaysia Vasudevan, creating a poignant expression of love. The song encapsulates the feelings of yearning and nostalgia, making it a perfect fit for romantic occasions. Set against the backdrop of Muthal Mariyathai, it enhances the emotional depth of the narrative. This classic melody continues to resonate with listeners, evoking memories of love and longing.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Poongatru Thirumbuma from Muthal Mariyathai is a timeless melody that beautifully captures the essence of love and longing. The enchanting voices of S. Janaki and Malaysia Vasudevan, combined with Ilaiyaraaja's masterful composition, create an unforgettable listening experience. This song resonates with anyone who has ever felt the bittersweet pangs of romance.
stanzaMeanings: Will the flowers return? Do they wish to come back? If I dance in the shade, will I get a mother's embrace?
Will the flowers return? Do they wish to come back?
Oh beloved, are you sad? Is the sky dark? Will this world not bear it? Is the sun angry?
What should I say? My heart cannot bear it. I cannot take it anymore.
Who is this pain for? There is no one in the town like you.
I sang a song for my sorrow, but I did not express my sadness.
Sweet melody, is it sadness? Sweet melody, is it sadness? Who can sing like that? Will the cuckoo show its face?
Will the flowers return? Do they wish to come back? If I dance in the shade, will I get a mother's embrace?
I cry inside and smile outside. I am just pretending to be good.
Your pretense is a little deadly; it should not reach our home.
Oh dear, my heart is like milk, sweet words. Come to my sight, oh girl.
I have sung a song, I have sung a song. Who is that in the flowers? Look closely, it’s the girl cuckoo.
Is that you, oh cuckoo? Whose cuckoo is that? I have forgotten the world in my heart.
Am I that cuckoo? Did the cuckoo come to my heart? I have forgotten the world.
